tasks.name: Environment Setup & DATE-LM Data Download
tasks.description: Install dependencies (trak, transformers, rank-bm25, scikit-learn, scipy). Download DATE-LM benchmark data for all 3 tasks. Download Pythia-70M and Pythia-1B checkpoints. Verify DATE-LM evaluation protocol works with a dummy attribution score. Register dataset/checkpoint paths in shared/registry.json.

pilot.pass_criteria: All dependencies install without error; DATE-LM data downloads and loads; both Pythia checkpoints load; dummy evaluation returns valid metric

tasks.name: Phase 0a: Pipeline Validation (RepSim + TRAK on Pythia-1B)
tasks.description: Run RepSim (last-layer cosine similarity) and TRAK (k=2048) on Pythia-1B for DATE-LM data_selection task with 100 samples. Validate that pipeline produces valid LDS scores and RepSim > TRAK as sanity check. Extract and cache last-layer representations for reuse.

pilot.pass_criteria: Both methods produce valid LDS scores in [0, 1]; RepSim LDS > TRAK LDS (sanity); runtime < 15 min per method

visualizations.description: Pipeline validation: RepSim vs TRAK LDS on pilot

tasks.name: Phase 0b: K-FAC Hessian Control on Pythia-70M (H6 -- CRITICAL)
tasks.description: Implement K-FAC full-eigendecomp IF on Pythia-70M. Run on DATE-LM data_selection task. Compare K-FAC IF LDS vs RepSim LDS. CRITICAL DECISION GATE: if gap < 5pp, the CRA thesis requires fundamental revision. This task uses Pythia-70M (d=512, ~70M params) where full eigendecomp is feasible.

pilot.pass_criteria: K-FAC IF produces valid LDS score; K-FAC IF LDS < RepSim LDS - 5pp (H6 not falsified). FALSIFICATION: K-FAC IF LDS >= RepSim LDS - 5pp triggers pivot to cand_b.

visualizations.description: K-FAC control: IF vs RepSim comparison

tasks.name: Phase 1: TRAK Standard (Cell A of 2x2 factorial)
tasks.description: Run TRAK (k=2048, standard scoring, no contrastive) on Pythia-1B for all 3 DATE-LM tasks. Compute LDS (data_selection), auPRC (toxicity_filtering), P@K (factual_attribution) with bootstrap CI (B=1000).

pilot.pass_criteria: Valid scores for all 3 tasks; LDS in [0, 1]; auPRC in [0, 1]; P@K in [0, 1]

visualizations.description: TRAK standard scores across DATE-LM tasks

tasks.name: Phase 1: TRAK Contrastive (Cell B of 2x2 factorial)
tasks.description: Run TRAK (k=2048) with contrastive scoring (mean-subtracted) on Pythia-1B for all 3 DATE-LM tasks. The contrastive score subtracts the mean attribution over the training pool. Bootstrap CI (B=1000).

pilot.pass_criteria: Valid scores for all 3 tasks; TRAK-C LDS > TRAK standard LDS (H2 directional check)

visualizations.description: TRAK contrastive scores across DATE-LM tasks

tasks.name: Phase 1: RepSim Standard (Cell C of 2x2 factorial)
tasks.description: Run RepSim (last-layer cosine similarity, standard scoring) on Pythia-1B for all 3 DATE-LM tasks. Cache extracted representations for reuse in contrastive and whitened variants. Bootstrap CI (B=1000).

pilot.pass_criteria: Valid scores for all 3 tasks; RepSim LDS > 0.3 (sanity); representations cached successfully

visualizations.description: RepSim standard scores across DATE-LM tasks

tasks.name: Phase 1: RepSim Contrastive (Cell D of 2x2 factorial)
tasks.description: Run RepSim with contrastive scoring (mean-subtracted cosine) on Pythia-1B for all 3 DATE-LM tasks. Reuse cached representations from repsim_standard. Bootstrap CI (B=1000).

pilot.pass_criteria: Valid scores for all 3 tasks; RepSim-C improvement over RepSim standard should be smaller than TRAK-C over TRAK standard (H2 asymmetry check)

visualizations.description: RepSim contrastive scores across DATE-LM tasks

tasks.name: Phase 1: Baselines (BM25, k-NN, DDA)
tasks.description: Run BM25 (rank-bm25), k-NN (cosine, k=50 in representation space), and DDA (parameter-space with debias+denoise) on Pythia-1B for DATE-LM tasks. BM25 and k-NN on all 3 tasks; DDA on data_selection only (most expensive). Bootstrap CI (B=1000).

pilot.pass_criteria: BM25 produces valid scores; k-NN produces valid scores; DDA produces valid LDS; BM25 LDS < RepSim LDS on data_selection (H8 directional check)

tasks.name: Phase 1: Factorial Analysis (H1, H2, H3, H8)
tasks.description: Aggregate results from all Phase 1 experiments. Compute: (1) H1 -- RepSim vs TRAK gap per task, (2) H2 -- contrastive gain asymmetry, (3) H3 -- 2-way ANOVA interaction term vs main effects, (4) H8 -- RepSim vs BM25 gap. Generate factorial result table and ANOVA statistics. Bootstrap CI on all comparisons.

pilot.pass_criteria: All hypothesis tests produce interpretable results; H1 gap >= 5pp on >= 2/3 tasks; H3 interaction < 30% of min main effect on >= 2/3 tasks

visualizations.description: ANOVA decomposition: main effects and interaction
Contrastive scoring gain by method and task

tasks.name: Phase 2a: Gradient & Representation Eigenspectrum (H4, H9)
tasks.description: On Pythia-70M: (1) Compute gradient covariance eigenspectrum using Lanczos iteration (top-500 eigenvalues) over DATE-LM data_selection training samples. (2) Compute exact representation covariance eigenspectrum (d=512). Measure r_eff(95%) for both. Compute condition numbers. H4: r_eff(Sigma_g) in [256, 1024]. H9: rep condition < 100, grad condition > 10^4.

pilot.pass_criteria: Lanczos converges (top-500 eigenvalues stable); r_eff(95%) computable; r_eff in [256, 1024] for gradients AND condition number ratio > 100x between spaces

visualizations.description: Spectral statistics: r_eff, condition number, explained variance

tasks.name: Phase 2b: TRAK Dimension Sweep (H5)
tasks.description: Run TRAK on Pythia-1B with projection dimensions k in {64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096} for DATE-LM data_selection task. Also run TRAK-PCA (project onto top-k eigenvectors of gradient covariance) at k in {256, 512, 1024, 2048} as smoking-gun test. Plot LDS vs k to identify saturation knee. H5: 90% of max LDS by k=2d=4096.
tasks.comment_gpu: 7 random projection dimensions + 4 PCA dimensions = 11 runs; parallelizing across 2 GPUs reduces wall time to ~60 min

pilot.pass_criteria: LDS monotonically non-decreasing with k (within noise); visible saturation knee exists; TRAK-PCA at k=d approaches RepSim performance (within 5pp)

visualizations.description: TRAK dimension sweep: LDS vs projection dimension k
TRAK random vs PCA projection comparison

tasks.name: Phase 2c: RepSim PCA Dimension Reduction Sweep
tasks.description: Run RepSim on Pythia-1B with PCA dimension reduction: k in {64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048} (full d=2048) for DATE-LM data_selection. Cross-validates H4 from representation side: performance should remain stable until k << d. Reuse cached representations.

pilot.pass_criteria: RepSim at k=d matches full RepSim; graceful degradation curve with knee at k ~ d/4 to d/2

visualizations.description: RepSim PCA dimension reduction: LDS vs reduced dimension

tasks.name: Phase 3a: Contrastive Scoring Universal Plug-in Matrix
tasks.description: Run 36-cell experiment matrix: {RepSim, TRAK, LoGra, DDA} x {standard, contrastive, whitened} x {data_selection, toxicity_filtering, factual_attribution} on Pythia-1B. For DDA, 'standard' is its built-in debias; 'contrastive' adds mean-subtraction on top. LoGra uses its structured projection. This validates contrastive scoring as a universal FM2 fix.
tasks.comment_gpu: 36 cells is large; many share cached representations/gradients. 2 GPUs allow parallel method evaluation. Exceeds 60 min because this is the comprehensive validation matrix -- splitting further would create excessive task overhead.

pilot.pass_criteria: Contrastive scoring improves >= 3 of 4 methods on >= 2 of 3 tasks; no method degrades by > 3pp with contrastive scoring

visualizations.description: Contrastive scoring gain heatmap: method x task

tasks.name: Phase 3b: Whitened Matched Filter Attribution (H7)
tasks.description: Implement whitened RepSim: phi^T Sigma_noise^{-1} psi. (1) Estimate noise covariance Sigma_noise from training representations using Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage. (2) Compute whitened attribution scores. (3) Compare whitened vs standard vs contrastive RepSim on all 3 DATE-LM tasks on Pythia-1B. (4) Compute per-query SNR_out for reliability estimation. H7: whitened > standard by 3-8pp on factual_attribution.

pilot.pass_criteria: Whitened RepSim produces valid scores (no NaN/Inf); whitened > standard on factual_attribution; Ledoit-Wolf converges; per-query SNR computable

visualizations.description: Whitened vs standard vs contrastive RepSim per task

decision_gates.action: PIVOT to cand_b (Hessian Quality Diagnosis); cancel all subsequent cand_a tasks
Revise orthogonality claim to 'correlated defects'; continue with cand_a but weaken narrative
Restrict CRA claims to data_selection and toxicity_filtering; discuss factual_attribution as limitation
